title: Riviera Branzino with Citrus & Herb Garden Salsa
description: Whole roasted branzino with a vibrant citrus-herb salsa inspired by Colagreco's Mirazur garden overlooking the Mediterranean. Bright lemon, orange, and fresh herbs celebrate the Riviera.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F. Score branzino 3 times on each side.
Stuff cavities with lemon slices, garlic, and a few basil leaves. Drizzle fish with 2 tbsp olive oil, season generously with salt and pepper.
Roast on a sheet pan for 22-25 minutes until skin is crispy and flesh flakes easily.
While fish roasts, make citrus salsa: combine diced orange segments, lemon juice, shallot, capers, parsley, remaining basil, mint, remaining olive oil, red pepper flakes, and a pinch of salt.
Plate fish and spoon citrus-herb salsa over the top. Serve immediately.

title: Garden Herb Lamb Chops with Flower Chimichurri
description: Mirazur-inspired lamb chops with a chimichurri brightened by edible flowers and garden herbs, bridging Colagreco's Argentine roots with his Riviera garden.
instructions: Make chimichurri: combine parsley, oregano, thyme, garlic, vinegar, lemon zest, red pepper flakes, and 1/4 cup olive oil. Season with 1/2 tsp salt. Set aside.
Pat lamb chops dry. Season generously with remaining salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or grill to high heat. Add remaining olive oil.
Sear lamb chops 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp 130F).
Rest 5 minutes, then serve topped with generous spoonfuls of chimichurri.

title: Open-Fire Spatchcock Chicken with Lemon & Wild Herbs
description: Inspired by Colagreco's Argentine open-fire traditions, this spatchcocked chicken is roasted with Riviera citrus and wild herbs for crispy skin and deep flavor.
instructions: Preheat oven to 450F (or prepare a hot grill for indirect cooking).
Rub spatchcocked chicken with olive oil, then season all over with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and dried oregano.
Place chicken skin-side up on a sheet pan. Tuck smashed garlic, rosemary, and thyme underneath and around the bird. Nestle lemon halves cut-side down alongside.
Roast 40-45 minutes until skin is deeply golden and thigh registers 165F.
Squeeze roasted lemon halves over chicken. Rest 10 minutes before carving.

title: Roasted Root Vegetable Bowl with Herb-Crusted Salmon
description: A Mirazur 'Universo' root-course tribute: earthy roasted roots topped with herb-crusted salmon. Inspired by Colagreco's biodynamic garden philosophy.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Toss root vegetables with 2 tbsp olive oil, cumin, 1 tsp salt, and pepper. Spread on a sheet pan.
Roast vegetables 20 minutes, then stir.
Mix remaining olive oil with garlic, dill, parsley, lemon zest, and remaining salt. Pat salmon dry and press herb mixture onto the top of each fillet.
Nestle salmon fillets among the roasted roots. Return to oven for 12-15 minutes until salmon is just cooked through.
Squeeze lemon juice over everything. Serve salmon atop the roots.

title: Patagonian Skirt Steak with Smoked Olive Oil & Herbs
description: Colagreco's Argentine heritage meets Riviera refinement: skirt steak with a smoked olive oil drizzle, charred scallions, and fresh herb salad.

ingredients.name: smoked olive oil
ingredients.notes: or regular olive oil with pinch smoked salt

instructions: Bring skirt steak to room temperature. Season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a grill or cast-iron skillet to very high heat. Sear steak 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
In the last minute, char scallions alongside the steak until wilted and lightly blackened.
Rest steak 5 minutes, then slice against the grain.
Toss parsley and cilantro with vinegar and regular olive oil for a quick herb salad.
Plate sliced steak with charred scallions and herb salad. Drizzle with smoked olive oil and squeeze lemon over the top.

title: Pan-Seared Sea Bass with Olive Tapenade & Fennel
description: A tribute to Mirazur's Menton seaside setting: delicate sea bass with Provencal olive tapenade and shaved fennel, Colagreco's Mediterranean soul on a plate.
instructions: Make tapenade: pulse olives, capers, anchovy (if using), garlic, and 1 tbsp olive oil in a food processor until coarsely chopped. Stir in basil.
Toss shaved fennel with lemon juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Set aside.
Pat sea bass dry. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat remaining olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Place fillets skin-side down, press gently for 30 seconds to prevent curling. Cook 4 minutes until skin is crispy.
Flip and cook 2-3 minutes more until just opaque through.
Plate fennel salad, top with sea bass skin-side up, and spoon olive tapenade alongside. Garnish with fennel fronds.

title: Leaf Course Chicken with Braised Greens & Salsa Verde
description: Inspired by Colagreco's Universo 'leaf' course: tender chicken thighs over braised garden greens with a vibrant Italian-Argentine salsa verde.
instructions: Season chicken thighs with 1 tsp salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Sear thighs skin-side down 6-7 minutes until golden, flip and cook 5 more minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook 30 seconds. Add kale, chard, and chicken broth. Cover and braise 5 minutes until wilted.
Return chicken to pan, nestling into greens. Cover and cook 10-12 minutes until chicken reaches 175F.
Make salsa verde: blend parsley, basil, minced garlic, anchovy, vinegar, and remaining olive oil until just combined but still chunky.
Serve chicken over braised greens with salsa verde drizzled generously on top.

title: Riviera Citrus-Cured Salmon with Avocado & Radish
description: Colagreco's love of citrus and the sea in one dish: quick-cured salmon with a bright citrus dressing, creamy avocado, and peppery radish.
instructions: Arrange salmon slices on a platter. Combine lemon, orange, and grapefruit juices with a pinch of salt. Pour over salmon and let cure 10-15 minutes.
Drain most of the citrus juice. Arrange avocado slices and radishes around and over the salmon.
Scatter shallot rings, dill, and chives over the platter.
Drizzle generously with olive oil. Season with flaky sea salt and cracked black pepper.
Serve immediately as a composed platter — elegant and bright.

title: Seed-Crusted Tuna with Roasted Pepper Sauce
description: Inspired by Colagreco's Universo 'seed' course: tuna steaks crusted in a vibrant seed mix, served over a smoky roasted red pepper sauce.
instructions: Blend roasted red peppers, garlic, smoked paprika, 1 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt until smooth. Set sauce aside.
Combine sesame seeds, sunflower seeds, and pumpkin seeds on a plate. Season tuna steaks with salt and pepper, then press into seed mixture on all sides.
Heat remaining olive oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear tuna 1-2 minutes per side for rare to medium-rare.
Spread roasted pepper sauce on plates. Top with a bed of arugula and place sliced tuna on top.
Serve immediately with any remaining sauce on the side.

title: Flower Course Turkey Meatballs in Saffron Tomato Broth
description: Colagreco's Universo 'flower' course reimagined: tender turkey meatballs simmered in a saffron-kissed tomato broth with zucchini and fresh herbs.
instructions: Mix turkey, almond flour, egg, half the garlic, oregano, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp pepper. Form into 16 meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown meatballs on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute onion until soft (3 minutes). Add remaining garlic and zucchini, cook 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, saffron with its water, remaining salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the pot. Cover and simmer 20 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Stir in torn basil. Serve in bowls with broth ladled generously over the meatballs.

title: Mirazur Herb Garden Frittata with Roasted Tomatoes
description: Inspired by Colagreco's legendary Mirazur garden: a loaded frittata with fresh herbs, roasted cherry tomatoes, and olives. Simple, elegant, garden-driven.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F. Whisk eggs with coconut cream, salt, pepper, chives, and thyme.
Heat olive oil in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Saute red onion 3 minutes, add garlic for 30 seconds.
Scatter cherry tomatoes and olives in the skillet. Pour egg mixture over the top.
Cook on stovetop 3-4 minutes until edges begin to set.
Transfer to oven and bake 15-18 minutes until puffed and golden.
Top with torn basil. Let cool 5 minutes before slicing into wedges.

title: Grilled Swordfish with Charred Lemon & Caper Relish
description: Bold and bright, this Colagreco-inspired swordfish pairs the Riviera's love of capers and citrus with Argentine grilling technique for a smoky, elegant dish.
instructions: Brush swordfish with 1 tbsp olive oil, season with salt, pepper, and dried oregano.
Grill over high heat 4-5 minutes per side until grill marks form and fish is opaque through. Grill lemon halves cut-side down until charred (2 minutes).
Make relish: combine cherry tomatoes, capers, garlic, parsley, lemon juice, and remaining olive oil. Toss gently.
Plate swordfish, squeeze charred lemon over the top, and spoon caper-tomato relish alongside.
Serve immediately with extra olive oil for drizzling.

title: Fruit Course Duck Breast with Fig & Red Wine Reduction
description: Colagreco's Universo 'fruit' course brought home: seared duck breast with a lush fig and red wine reduction, roasted sweet potatoes, and fresh thyme.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Toss sweet potatoes with olive oil, 1/2 tsp salt, and thyme. Roast 25-30 minutes until caramelized.
Score duck skin in a crosshatch pattern. Season generously with remaining salt and pepper.
Place duck breasts skin-side down in a cold skillet. Turn heat to medium and render fat for 8-10 minutes until skin is deeply crispy. Flip and cook 3-4 minutes for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes.
Pour off all but 1 tbsp duck fat from the pan. Saute shallots 2 minutes. Add figs, red wine, and broth. Simmer 8-10 minutes until reduced by half and figs are plump. Stir in balsamic vinegar.
Slice duck breast. Serve over roasted sweet potatoes with fig-wine sauce spooned generously over the top.

title: Mediterranean Beef Stew with Olives & Orange Zest
description: Colagreco's Riviera-meets-Argentina in a hearty stew: braised beef with olives, tomatoes, and a signature orange zest brightness from Menton's famed citrus.

ingredients.name: orange
ingredients.notes: zested, peel in strips for braising

instructions: Preheat oven to 325F. Pat beef dry and season with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over high heat. Brown beef in batches, 3-4 minutes per side. Set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Saute onion 4 minutes, then add garlic and carrots for 2 minutes.
Deglaze with red wine, scraping up fond. Add crushed tomatoes, broth, olives, orange peel strips, rosemary, and thyme.
Return beef to pot. Bring to a simmer, then cover and transfer to oven.
Braise 2 hours until beef is fork-tender. Remove herb sprigs and orange peel.
Serve in bowls garnished with fresh orange zest.

title: Cod with Sea Vegetables & Ginger-Citrus Broth
description: Colagreco's fascination with sea vegetables meets Riviera cod: flaky white fish in a light ginger-citrus broth with wakame and fresh herbs.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wide, deep skillet over medium heat. Gently saute garlic and ginger 1 minute until fragrant.
Add fish broth, coconut aminos, lemon juice, and lime juice. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Season cod with salt and white pepper. Nestle fillets into the broth. Add bok choy around the fish.
Cover and poach 10-12 minutes until cod flakes easily and bok choy is tender.
Add rehydrated wakame in the last 2 minutes to warm through.
Ladle broth, vegetables, and fish into shallow bowls. Top with scallions and a drizzle of olive oil.

title: Chimichurri Cauliflower Steaks with Grilled Chicken
description: Colagreco's vegetable-forward philosophy with Argentine boldness: thick cauliflower steaks charred and draped in chimichurri, paired with grilled chicken for protein.
instructions: Make chimichurri: combine parsley, oregano, garlic, vinegar, red pepper flakes, and half the olive oil. Season with 1/2 tsp salt.
Brush cauliflower steaks with 1 tbsp olive oil, season with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Brush with remaining olive oil.
Grill or pan-sear cauliflower steaks over medium-high heat, 5-6 minutes per side until charred and tender.
Grill chicken 5-6 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 165F. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Plate cauliflower steaks topped with sliced chicken and generous chimichurri.

title: Menton Lemon Roasted Chicken Thighs with Artichokes
description: A tribute to Menton's famous lemons and Colagreco's Mirazur terroir: crispy chicken thighs braised with artichoke hearts, olives, and abundant lemon in the Riviera style.

ingredients.name: artichoke hearts
ingredients.notes: canned or frozen, drained and quartered

instructions: Preheat oven to 425F. Pat chicken thighs dry and season generously with salt, pepper, and dried oregano.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken skin-side down for 6-7 minutes until deeply golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add garlic, artichoke hearts, and olives. Cook 2 minutes. Add chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping up any fond.
Nestle chicken thighs skin-side up into the skillet among the artichokes. Tuck lemon slices, thyme, and rosemary around the chicken.
Transfer to oven and roast 25-30 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and thighs reach 175F.
Drizzle with remaining olive oil and serve straight from the skillet.
